label: Squarish 2BR / 2 baths — ensuite + common share a plumbing wall; bath block sits BETWEEN master (front) and standard (rear). Open-plan great_room + kitchen.

adjacencies.note: ensuite is master's exclusive — door opens from master, no other way in
shared plumbing wall between the two baths — keeps all wet stacks in one bay
common touches standard's south face — the bath block acts as the acoustic buffer between bedrooms
standard door — 1.2 m floor gives 0.8 m clear door + frame on each side
common is publicly accessible (used by standard via the great_room; also a guest WC)
great_room + kitchen are one open LDK (gr style)

soft_proximities.note: secondary pull — keeps the master-adjacent side of the wet core close to master

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_clustered_baths
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Soft floors are tiered by priority. auto_apply (always): kitchen width floored at 1.8 m — still a usable galley (above PD 1096's 1.5 m hard minimum) and the lowest the solver may shrink kitchen to. preferred_apply (tried first, relaxed on infeasibility together as a group): baths at 3.0 m² preferred-low AND kitchen at 2.0 m generous galley width. On the tightest lots the runner relaxes the whole preferred group, surfacing a 'tiered_preferred_dropped' warning. Bath priority is implicit in this split: even when preferred relaxes, kitchen never drops below 1.8 m — but baths CAN drop toward the 1.2 m² hard min if absolutely necessary.

notes: v2 catalog topology #2 — 2BR / 2 baths CLUSTERED. Covers the 65-72 m² floor
band (PH mid-market, one rung above single_bath_gr).
Program: 2BR with master ensuite + common bath. Both baths share a plumbing
wall (wet_core). The bath BLOCK sits between master (front) and standard
(rear) — it functions as the acoustic+privacy buffer between sleeping spaces.
Common bath is publicly accessible via a door to the great_room so guests
and standard use it without going through master.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_preference='right'):
Front: [master              | great_room                          ]
Mid:   [ensuite | common    | great_room                          ]
Rear:  [standard            | kitchen                             ]
Both ensuite and common sit in the MIDDLE band (y between master.y_end and
standard.y_start). They share a vertical plumbing wall (wet_core).
Ensuite is on the LEFT (master side), common on the RIGHT (great-room side).
Standard sits at the rear, accessed from the great_room via a 1.2 m+ shared
wall (gives door clearance + frame on each side).
Zone classification per v2 convention: both ensuite and common are 'private'
(spatially they sit inside the private wing — the wet core IS the bath
block between bedrooms). Kitchen is 'public' (open-plan with great_room).
The 55/45 private/public ratio constraint then balances the wing distribution.
Carport: 3.0 m wide x 6.0 m deep (Item 2 default single carport). The 1 m
width + 4 m depth that exceed the setbacks become a building_void on the
front-right corner — same L-shape pattern as topology #1.
When the brief sets carport_preference='left', the runner mirrors the
topology so the wet-core bay flips to the other side.
